data.description:
## Problem
Run in a backgrounded shell whose cwd is reset away from the repo, 'rebar review-plan' can complete the review but resolve repo/tracker root from the transient cwd, then fail to persist an attestation WITHOUT surfacing an error to the caller — a workflow that backgrounds gate runs appears to succeed while signing nothing.

## Investigation target
Trace review-plan repo/tracker-root resolution and the sign step's error handling for a silently-swallowed failure when cwd != repo root. Reproduce at runtime before fixing.

## Fix (report ask)
Fail loudly when signing cannot persist, and resolve repo/tracker root independently of the transient cwd (or require an explicit root and error if unresolved).

## Acceptance criteria
- A review-plan sign that cannot persist surfaces a non-zero exit / explicit error to the caller (never a silent no-op success).
- Root resolution does not silently depend on a cwd that differs from the ticket's repo.

## Verification
Runtime repro of the cwd-reset scenario + a regression test asserting the loud failure.
data.title: review-plan from a cwd-reset/background shell can sign nothing with no error surfaced
